title: Standardize one error envelope
description: Return all errors in a single structured shape with a stable machine-readable code, a message, and details, so clients handle them uniformly.
body: Use when defining error responses for any endpoint. Do not let different endpoints return errors in different shapes. Actions: define one envelope with a stable error code, human message, and optional field-level details; map status codes consistently; document the code list. Validation signals include a client parsing every error the same way and switching on the code rather than the message text. Risk boundary: inconsistent error shapes force brittle client parsing and leak internals, so adopt one envelope and keep messages free of sensitive detail.
